description: Golden dataset for risk scoring validation with 50+ occupations across 6 categories and 5 risk bands

occupations.title: Software Developers, Applications
occupations.rationale: High AI exposure (coding assistance, code generation), but creative/architectural work remains human. Layer 1 research score: 78.

occupations.title: Computer Programmers
occupations.rationale: Very high automation potential for routine coding tasks. AI pair programming tools already replacing basic work. Layer 1: 80.

occupations.title: Data Scientists
occupations.rationale: AI tools automate data cleaning, model selection, but domain expertise and business insight remain critical. Layer 1: 85.

occupations.title: Computer Systems Analysts
occupations.rationale: System design and requirements gathering partially automatable. Layer 1: 82.

occupations.title: Information Security Analysts
occupations.rationale: AI assists threat detection but strategic security decisions require human judgment. Layer 1: 76.

occupations.title: Network and Computer Systems Administrators
occupations.rationale: Automation handles routine tasks, but complex troubleshooting needs human expertise. Layer 1: 72.

occupations.title: Web Developers
occupations.rationale: Low-code/no-code tools and AI code generation rapidly automating basic web development. Layer 1: 88.

occupations.title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
occupations.rationale: Leadership, team coordination, and strategic IT planning resist automation. Layer 1: 52.

occupations.title: Registered Nurses
occupations.rationale: Physical care, emotional support, complex clinical judgment - core nursing tasks resist automation. Layer 1: 35.

occupations.title: Family Medicine Physicians
occupations.rationale: Diagnostic support from AI but patient relationships, complex cases need human physicians. Layer 1: 28.

occupations.title: Physician Assistants
occupations.rationale: AI diagnostic tools augment but cannot replace hands-on patient care and clinical decisions. Layer 1: 38.

occupations.title: Occupational Therapists
occupations.rationale: Highly personalized physical therapy requiring human adaptation and empathy. Layer 1: 32.

occupations.title: Physical Therapists
occupations.rationale: Physical assessment and hands-on treatment planning require human touch and judgment. Layer 1: 34.

occupations.title: Home Health Aides
occupations.rationale: Hands-on personal care in varied home environments. Physical and emotional work. Layer 1: 25.

occupations.title: Medical Assistants
occupations.rationale: Some administrative tasks automatable, but patient interaction and clinical support remain. Layer 1: 40.

occupations.title: Licensed Practical Nurses
occupations.rationale: Mix of routine care (automatable documentation) and hands-on patient care (not automatable). Layer 1: 42.

occupations.title: Phlebotomists
occupations.rationale: Physical skill drawing blood, but data entry and scheduling automatable. Layer 1: 48.

occupations.title: Electricians
occupations.rationale: Physical installation work in unpredictable environments. Safety-critical, manual dexterity required. Layer 1: 15.

occupations.title: Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
occupations.rationale: Physical work in varied, unpredictable situations requiring problem-solving and manual skill. Layer 1: 12.

occupations.title: Carpenters
occupations.rationale: Precision manual work adapting to unique construction contexts. Layer 1: 20.

occupations.title: Construction Laborers
occupations.rationale: Heavy physical labor in dynamic, outdoor environments. Layer 1: 18.

occupations.title: Maintenance and Repair Workers, General
occupations.rationale: Varied repair tasks requiring diagnostic problem-solving in unpredictable conditions. Layer 1: 22.

occupations.title: Operating Engineers and Other Construction Equipment Operators
occupations.rationale: Heavy equipment operation with safety judgments in dynamic construction sites. Layer 1: 16.

occupations.title: Automotive Service Technicians and Mechanics
occupations.rationale: Diagnostic software assists, but hands-on repair work in varied vehicle conditions requires expertise. Layer 1: 24.

occupations.title: Industrial Machinery Mechanics
occupations.rationale: Complex machinery diagnosis and physical repair. Some monitoring automatable. Layer 1: 21.

occupations.title: Graphic Designers
occupations.rationale: AI design tools augment work but creative direction, client relationships remain human. Layer 1: 65.

occupations.title: Writers and Authors
occupations.rationale: AI writing tools create drafts but original voice, storytelling, editing need human creativity. Layer 1: 72.

occupations.title: Multimedia Artists and Animators
occupations.rationale: AI can generate assets but artistic vision, character development are human skills. Layer 1: 58.

occupations.title: Interpreters and Translators
occupations.rationale: Machine translation handles basic text, but nuanced cultural/contextual translation needs humans. Layer 1: 70.

occupations.title: Producers and Directors
occupations.rationale: Creative vision, team leadership, artistic decisions resist automation. AI assists post-production. Layer 1: 42.

occupations.title: Interior Designers
occupations.rationale: AI tools for visualization, but client relationships and spatial creativity remain human. Layer 1: 56.

occupations.title: Film and Video Editors
occupations.rationale: AI editing tools automate cuts, but storytelling rhythm and creative choices are human. Layer 1: 62.

occupations.title: Public Relations Specialists
occupations.rationale: AI generates press releases but relationship building and crisis management need humans. Layer 1: 64.

occupations.title: Chief Executives
occupations.rationale: Strategic decision-making, stakeholder relationships, leadership - fundamentally human. Layer 1: 38.

occupations.title: Marketing Managers
occupations.rationale: AI tools for analytics and targeting, but strategic brand decisions require human judgment. Layer 1: 48.

occupations.title: Medical and Health Services Managers
occupations.rationale: Complex healthcare operations, regulatory compliance, and patient care oversight. Layer 1: 35.

occupations.title: Financial Managers
occupations.rationale: AI handles analysis and forecasting, but strategic financial decisions and risk management need humans. Layer 1: 55.

occupations.title: Management Analysts
occupations.rationale: Data analysis automatable but strategic recommendations and change management are human. Layer 1: 65.

occupations.title: Administrative Services Managers
occupations.rationale: Operations coordination and team management. Some admin tasks automatable. Layer 1: 52.

occupations.title: Food Service Managers
occupations.rationale: Staff coordination, quality control, customer service - human-centric management. Layer 1: 42.

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts
occupations.rationale: Data analysis highly automatable but strategic insights and recommendations need human interpretation. Layer 1: 72.

occupations.title: Retail Salespersons
occupations.rationale: Self-checkout and AI recommendations automate some tasks, but human interaction still valued. Layer 1: 45.

occupations.title: Fast Food and Counter Workers
occupations.rationale: Automated ordering exists but food preparation and customer service remain largely manual. Layer 1: 22.

occupations.title: Waiters and Waitresses
occupations.rationale: Personal service, menu recommendations, customer interaction are inherently human. Layer 1: 20.

occupations.title: Cooks, Restaurant
occupations.rationale: Some automated cooking equipment, but recipe adaptation and quality judgment need humans. Layer 1: 24.

occupations.title: Hairdressers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists
occupations.rationale: Highly manual, creative, client-relationship-driven work. Layer 1: 18.

occupations.title: Childcare Workers
occupations.rationale: Emotional care, supervision, child development - fundamentally human work. Layer 1: 15.

occupations.title: Customer Service Representatives
occupations.rationale: Chatbots handle routine queries, but complex problem-solving and empathy remain human. Layer 1: 75.

occupations.title: First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers
occupations.rationale: Team management, conflict resolution, training are human skills. Some scheduling automatable. Layer 1: 42.

occupations.title: Office Clerks, General
occupations.rationale: Very high automation potential - data entry, filing, scheduling are core AI strengths. Layer 1: 88.

occupations.title: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks
occupations.rationale: Routine bookkeeping tasks highly automatable with accounting software and AI. Layer 1: 90.

occupations.title: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants
occupations.rationale: Scheduling, document preparation, data entry - all prime automation targets. Layer 1: 92.

occupations.title: Accountants and Auditors
occupations.rationale: Routine bookkeeping automatable, but professional judgment, auditing, advisory less so. Layer 1: 70.

occupations.title: Financial Analysts
occupations.rationale: Financial modeling automated but strategic investment decisions need human insight. Layer 1: 68.

occupations.title: Elementary School Teachers
occupations.rationale: Child development, classroom management, emotional support - core teaching is human. Layer 1: 32.

occupations.title: Secondary School Teachers
occupations.rationale: Content delivery can be augmented by AI but mentorship and student engagement are human. Layer 1: 35.

occupations.title: Heavy and Tractor-Trailer Truck Drivers
occupations.rationale: Autonomous vehicles advancing but regulatory, safety, last-mile challenges remain. Layer 1: 58.

occupations.title: Lawyers
occupations.rationale: Legal research and document review automatable, but courtroom advocacy and client counseling remain human. Layer 1: 62.

occupations.title: Paralegals and Legal Assistants
occupations.rationale: Document review, legal research, filing - highly automatable with AI legal tools. Layer 1: 88.

occupations.title: Police and Sheriff's Patrol Officers
occupations.rationale: Judgment in unpredictable situations, community relationships, crisis intervention are human. Layer 1: 25.

occupations.title: Firefighters
occupations.rationale: Physical emergency response, life-safety decisions, teamwork in dynamic situations. Layer 1: 18.
